title: OpenClaw device token re-pairing computed caller-scope containment from request scopes, so an empty scope set bypassed the guard
description: In the gateway device token rotation handler (src/gateway/server-methods/devices.ts) the caller-scope containment check derives requestedScopes from the request via normalizeDeviceAuthScopes(scopes ?? pairedDevice.tokens?.[normalizedRole]?.scopes ?? pairedDevice.scopes) and passes them to resolveMissingRequestedScope({ role, requestedScopes, allowedScopes: authz.callerScopes }). resolveMissingRequestedScope iterates over requestedScopes and returns null when the set is empty, so a re-pairing/rotation request with an empty scope set produces no missing scope and the containment guard is skipped. rotateDeviceToken({ deviceId, role, scopes }) is then invoked and the persisted token falls back to the device's existing approved scope baseline, restoring/retaining scopes broader than the empty-scope caller should be able to grant. The sibling revoke handler called revokeDeviceToken({ deviceId, role }) with no caller-scope containment at all.
